mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with a p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and housing starts indicates a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, which reduces near-term corporate revenues and produces negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.05, or the interaction term coefficient is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The positive correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns reverses or becomes statistically insignificant during recovery phases, with a Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth expands aggregate corporate profit pools and improves long-term earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant relationship with 60-day forward returns, or the predictive R-squared remains below 0.01 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.5% monthly threshold forces markets to price in tighter monetary policy and erodes real corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.5%, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and retail sales confirms a healthy labor-income feedback loop that sustains consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to outperform a baseline market model, with an information ratio below 0.3 over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and reduced ancillary consumer spending, which depresses corporate earnings forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.05, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 50% in late-cycle environments.
